info.name: FlexCount SCIM API
info.description:
Test collection for the FlexCount SCIM API (repo: WIS_SCIMApp_RestAPI).

SETUP
1. Import an environment file from the same folder.
2. Fill in `scim_token` yourself — it ships blank on purpose. Get it from Key Vault, secret name `ScimToken`, in that environment's vault.
3. Do NOT commit a real token back into the environment file.

ORDER
Run folders top to bottom. The create tests make the users the update tests need.

EXPECTATIONS
Every expectation here was verified against the code, not against the older Word test guides — those are out of date and contradict each other.
Where the code does something surprising, the test says so and points at a gap ID in docs/06-known-gaps.md.

MOST IMPORTANT TESTS
- TC-N2: proves the region guard works. If it fails, stop and escalate.
- TC-N1: the only test that exercises region de-duplication.
- TC-18a/b/c: Entra Test Connection. Any failure blocks all provisioning.

See docs/04-testing.md for full detail and the SQL to verify each case.

item.name: 00 - Discovery (no auth)

exec: // NOTE: advertises etag.supported = true, but meta.version is always W/"" — gap G-04/G-05

request.description: Anonymous by design — Entra reads this before authenticating.

item.description: Entra reads these before authenticating. They must work anonymously.

exec: // DB: Id_Role = 1, Feature 23 -> Permission 6, Feature 24 -> Permission 6, no User_Region rows
// KNOWN GAP G-03: roles[0].value comes back as "" not "Admin"
pm.test("roles[0].value is empty (known gap G-03)", () => pm.expect(j.roles[0].value).to.eql(""));

request.description: Creates an Admin. Verify in DB: Id_Role=1, Feature 24 enabled (Permission 6), no regions.

exec: // DB: Id_Role = 2 (NOT 1). This is the HQ prefix-collision test.
// If Id_Role comes back as 1, the HQ check order in ResolveRoles has been broken.

request.description: Corporate, not Admin. Corporate-User- is a prefix of Corporate-User-HQ- so order matters.

exec: // DB: Primary_Phone = 1-612-619-0003 (the mobile — because primary:true decides on CREATE)
// NOTE: on PATCH the buckets are decided by type, not the flag. See gap G-07.

request.description: Regional with one group. Regions come from the DB via SearchCustomerGroups.

exec: // DB: Primary_Phone = 1-612-619-9001 (the WORK one — PATCH uses type)
// KNOWN GAP G-07: Primary_Phone_Type is NOT updated. Stays whatever it was.

request.description: Name + phone only. Role and status must not change.

exec: // This also proves stringified-JSON role parsing works.
// A 400 here means the TrimStart().StartsWith("{") block in ResolveRoles is broken.

request.description: Stringified JSON role format — this is what Entra actually sends on PATCH.

exec: // DB: 195 regions gone, 394 regions in place. Full replace, not merge.
// DB: user row, regions and permissions ALL preserved
// KNOWN GAP G-06: response body has empty name and role — it is a throwaway object
// DB: Status back to Active, Is_Active back to 1, NO duplicate row
// Order matters: if active:false appears anywhere the deactivate branch wins.

request.description: active + roles together, in one PATCH. This is what Entra sends on reassignment.

exec: // DB: Id_Role 3 -> 2, ALL User_Region rows deleted
// This is the case where the region guard does NOT fire — correct, because
// the user is no longer Regional so empty regions mean wipe. Compare TC-N2.
// ApplyPatch builds ONE update object from all 4 ops and sends one PUT.
// There is no way to half-apply it.

item.name: TC-17b Multi-op: name + phone + two Regional groups

exec: // The two Entra id GUIDs are parsed and discarded — ScimRole has no Id property.
// primary is NOT read — swap the flags and the answer is the same.
// CHECK THE TRACE: SearchCustomerGroups must NOT be called.
// If it is, the priority logic has regressed.
// ONE SearchCustomerGroups call with both names, not two calls.
// Do NOT assert ordering — the SQL has no ORDER BY. Assert the set.

item.name: TC-24 Add a second group to an existing Regional user

exec: // Entra sends the FULL desired state, not a delta. Both groups.

item.name: TC-25 Remove one group from a dual-group user

exec: // Entra sends only the REMAINING group.
// DB: back to 7 rows. Group394 values gone entirely.
// Proves AddOrUpdateUserRegions is wipe-and-replace, not additive.
// This one DOES hit the Customer API. It works because IsEmailValidOrGuid() accepts a GUID.
// Any error here blocks Entra provisioning entirely.

request.description: Entra Test Connection. MUST return 200 + empty list.

exec: pm.test("detail matches code", () => pm.expect(j.detail).to.eql("Error resolving roles: No valid entitlement roles found."));
// RFC 7644 says this should be invalidValue. Entra only reads the status code.
// NO user is created — ResolveRoles runs before the dup check and before any write.

item.name: TC-26 Empty roles array on patch -> 400

exec: pm.expect(j.detail).to.eql("Role validation failed: No valid entitlement roles found in the provided in roles array."));
// Yes, "in the provided in roles array" — the message has a typo. Matched exactly on purpose.

item.name: TC-27 Unrecognised role + name change -> 400, nothing applied

exec: pm.test("detail matches code", () => pm.expect(j.detail).to.eql("Role validation failed: No valid entitlement roles found."));
// VERIFY IN DB: givenName is NOT "ShouldNotUpdate".
// Atomicity comes from the controller returning early, not from a transaction.

item.name: 08 - Extra cases (not in the old guides)

exec: // Every other multi-group test uses 195 + 394, which have ZERO overlap.
// So the de-duplication code has never once been exercised.
// 394 and 398 BOTH contain region 300 and market 47.
// DB expectation — each value appears ONCE:
// Duplicates here mean MergeRegions GroupBy has broken.

request.description: The only test that exercises region de-duplication.

item.name: TC-N2 Name-only patch on a REGIONAL user (region guard)

exec: // TC-04 does the same thing on an ADMIN — who has no regions to lose.
// This is the ONLY test that proves the region guard works.
// No roles in the patch -> ParsedGroupNames empty -> region lookup skipped
// -> Region1..4 serialise as [] -> UpdateUserCommandHandler guard catches it:
//      if (!becomingRegional || hasIncomingRegions)   // false || false = false -> SKIP
// VERIFY IN DB: all 7 region rows STILL THERE.
// IF THE REGIONS VANISH, STOP AND ESCALATE.
// That guard has been removed or broken, and every Regional user will lose
// their market access on their next name change. That is a production incident.

request.description: Protects against silent region wipe. Run this on every release.

item.name: TC-N3 Patch with a group that is not in the database

exec: // CURRENT BEHAVIOUR (known gap G-01): 200 OK, old regions kept.
pm.test("200 OK — documents the CURRENT fail-open (gap G-01)", () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
// Group999 matches the Group\d+ regex so it is accepted as Regional.
// SearchCustomerGroups finds nothing -> Customer API 404 -> we return an empty list
// -> empty regions -> guard skips -> user keeps their OLD access. Entra logs success.
// Only trace: "no matching groups found" at Information level.
// VERIFY IN DB: the old regions are unchanged.
// WHEN G-01 IS FIXED: change this to expect 400 and update 04-testing.md.

request.description: Documents the fail-open. This is the most likely way the system silently does the wrong thing.

item.description: Three cases that find real bugs. Add them to your regression run.

exec: // URL-encode the emails so they can be used as {id} in the path
const enc = (k) => { const v = pm.environment.get(k); if (v) pm.environment.set(k + '_enc', encodeURIComponent(v)); };
console.warn('scim_token is empty. Get it from Key Vault (secret: ScimToken) and paste it into the environment.');
